source: apify/instagram-scraper (own account: manual fallback — Instagram blocks scraper access to this small account)

own.bio: Strange but true stories, told quietly. New odd hour, every week.

posts.caption: In 2021, one engineer typed one wrong command and 3.5 billion people lost the internet in a single instant. Not hacked. Not attacked. Just one wrong line of code during a routine maintenance check. Facebook's own engineers couldn't even log in to fix it, their access tools were on the same servers that just vanished. They had to physically drive to data centers and override servers by hand. One by one. 6 hours. $60 million lost every single hour. And the exact same vulnerability? Still exists right now. The internet is more fragile than you think.

2021 mein ek engineer ne ek galat command type ki aur 3.5 billion logon ka internet ek pal mein band ho gaya. Hack nahi hua. Attack nahi hua. Bas ek routine maintenance check ke dauran ek galat line of code. 6 ghante. Har ghante 60 million dollar ka nuksaan. Internet utna strong nahi jitna hum sochte hain.

There is a sound that only 2% of people can hear. Scientists have studied it for 50 years and still have no answer. It's called The Hum — and the leading theory is that it's the Earth itself vibrating at a frequency most humans aren't built to detect. A woman heard it for 11 years, moved across the country to escape it, and it followed her to the new house. Stop right now, close your eyes, and listen. What do you hear?

We call space the final frontier. But we've barely explored our own planet. Earth is stranger than anything we've found out there — and most people have no idea. From perfect solar eclipses to oceans deeper than mountains are tall, this is the planet you thought you knew.

In 1974, a man calculated the exact day he would die and spent a year preparing for it. What happened next is stranger than the prediction itself. Frank was obsessed with numerology. He told his family, quit his job, and gave away everything he owned. When the day came and went without incident, he thought he'd beaten fate. He hadn't.

They had to physically drive to data centers and override servers by hand. One by one. 6 hours. $60 million lost every single hour. And the exact same vulnerability? Still exists right now. The internet is more fragile than you think.

recentPosts.caption:
You've heard about synchronized swimming, but have you heard about synchronized rocket launching?

Don't miss out, and join us on this space adventure! Get Star Birds, which we developed together with our friends at @toukana_interactive on Steam!
What exactly is caffeine, and how does it affect your brain? Its special chemistry is what makes it helpful in keeping you focused, but it also comes with a cost.
Caption this Nightshift scene
Yesterday we talked about the top 3 microbe predators, and today we share the top 3 memes from that video 😌

There is a hidden world filled with monsters you never heard of, from predators who spit acid and shoot harpoons, to armored hunters that devour their prey alive! Deep in the oceans, and even in fresh water from puddles and lakes, these microscopic super predators remained hidden in plain sight, and evolved over the last 2 billion years.

What are the deadliest predators of the microworld, and what kind of bizarre biological weapons have they evolved over the past billions of years? Watch our fu
When someone opens snacks in the office
What could 1% of the richest countries’ income achieve? From malaria vaccines and clean water to renewable energy, a surprisingly small fraction of global wealth could transform millions of lives.

Your favorite shirt of the summer now comes with a very special souvenir from space!

Get a free set of Space Tour Patches when you buy a Space Tour Shirt, this summer* ✨
We teamed up with artist @haxloeffler to create this exclusive, two-sided shirt design featuring a cosmic throwback to our most iconic space videos. Suit up, secure your free patches, and browse a few more cosmic essentials for the journey.

*Starting from 23.06.2026 until 07.07.2026, when you purchase the Space Tour Shirt in t

Could YOU survive a royal marriage? Henry VIII had six wives, but becoming Queen of England is only the beginning of the challenge. To survive this, you have to navigate religious upheaval, political games, shifting alliances, and the unpredictable moods of one of history's most notorious monarchs. Some lost their crowns, and some lost their lives, but only one outlived the king.

Who were Henry VIII's six wives, and why was marrying him a death sentence for most of them? And what about you, cou
Yep, it do be like that sometimes... 💀

This is the deadliest virus on our planet… However, human cells are safe. The deadliest virus on our planet only feasts on bacteria, hence its name: Bacteriophage - bacteria eater. Now that our antibiotics aren't as effective against the ever-evolving multiresistant bacteria, phages are a promising tool for defending ourselves in the future. They are highly specialized so they only attack the bacteria they’re supposed to, reducing the side effects of a treatment.

If the Moon tasted like cheese, we are sure this asteroid would taste like an orange-flavored jellybean!

Have you already started your space adventure? Star Birds, which we developed together with our friends at Toukana Interactive, is available on Steam!

competitors.bio: Discover the most incredible experiences from around the world 🌎✈️ Business: info@damiermedia.com

recentPosts.caption:
For more than 50 years, Liu Guojiang carved a staircase by hand into a remote mountain in Chongqing, China, all for the woman he loved.⁠
⁠
This mission soon became a lifelong purpose as he continued to shape the rock step by step, year after year, in complete isolation.⁠
⁠
Today, the “Love Staircase” stretches over 6,000 steps, standing as one of the most powerful symbols of devotion and endurance ever built by hand.⁠
⁠
By: @city_news_service, Imaginechina, China Bulletin

For the Mundari people of South Sudan, cattle are much more than animals.⁠
⁠
They symbolize wealth, family, tradition, and identity, shaping nearly every aspect of daily life. From marriages to ceremonies, cattle play a central role in the community, and many families spend their lives caring for herds passed down through generations.⁠
⁠
Even the ash from burned cow dung has a purpose, helping protect both people and cattle from insects in the hot climate.⁠
⁠
By: @africageo, South Sudan Safari T

Kawah Ijen is famous for its rare electric-blue flames, created when sulfuric gases ignite as they escape the crater at extreme temperatures. In the dark, the glowing flow can look like icy violet lava moving through the landscape.⁠
⁠
But by sunrise, the illusion fades, revealing a turquoise acidic lake surrounded by thick volcanic smoke.⁠
⁠
It’s one of the most beautiful and dangerous natural phenomena on Earth.⁠
⁠
By: @geologyin

What looks like a glowing ocean straight out of a dream is actually created by some of the smallest creatures in the sea.⁠
⁠
These are sea fireflies, tiny bioluminescent organisms found mainly in Japan’s Seto Inland Sea. When disturbed or during mating, they release a glowing blue light that turns the shoreline into what looks like liquid stars.⁠
⁠
The light is not just for beauty. It helps them communicate, attract mates, and even escape predators by creating a brief flash in the water.⁠
⁠
Indi

This is one of the most exclusive travel experiences in Japan.⁠
⁠
The Seven Stars in Kyushu is a luxury sleeper train with only 10 suites onboard, designed more like a boutique hotel than a form of transport.⁠
⁠
Launched in 2013, it travels across Kyushu, Japan’s southern island, offering views of mountains, coastlines, hot springs, and traditional villages along the way.⁠
⁠
Because demand is so high, guests usually need to win a lottery just for the chance to book a journey.⁠
⁠
By: @sevenstars_

Najin and Fatu, the world’s last two northern white rhinos, live under 24/7 protection in Kenya. Their dedicated caretakers know their personalities, routines, and behaviors inside out, providing constant care that goes far beyond security.

Every day is focused on protecting these critically important animals while ensuring they live as comfortably and stress-free as possible. It’s a powerful reminder of the commitment required to help preserve a species on the edge of extinction.

📷 by @safar

In 2003, researchers at Tibet’s Sakya Monastery made an astonishing discovery.⁠
⁠
Hidden behind a sealed wall was a vast chamber containing around 84,000 ancient manuscripts that had remained untouched for centuries.⁠
⁠
Stretching roughly 60 meters long and 10 meters high, the hidden library preserved a remarkable collection of religious, historical, and philosophical texts, offering a rare glimpse into centuries of knowledge and scholarship.⁠
⁠
Many of the manuscripts are still being studied to

In China, a fully functional convenience store has been built in one of the most extreme and hard-to-reach locations imaginable, surrounded by towering cliffs and dramatic mountain landscapes.⁠
⁠
What makes it so fascinating is the contrast: a place designed for quick stops, placed somewhere that requires real effort just to reach.⁠
⁠
It has everything you’d expect from a normal store, but getting there is the real challenge.⁠
⁠
By: @archdigest @mustsharenews @chinadailynews

In the Faroe Islands, Sørvágsvatn (also known as Leitisvatn) creates one of the most famous optical illusions in the world.⁠
⁠
From certain viewpoints, it appears as if the lake is floating high above the Atlantic Ocean, defying gravity itself.⁠
⁠
But the truth is, the lake sits on a cliff only about 30 meters above sea level. The dramatic cliffs and perspective from the ocean below create the illusion.⁠
⁠
By: @tomas_malco_malik @the_real_avh

One of the most unusual chess performances in history took place in St. Petersburg, where an entire match was brought to life using real people instead of chess pieces.⁠
⁠
Thirty-two participants stood on a giant board, each acting as a living piece, moving only when instructed as the game unfolded like a live theatrical performance.⁠
⁠
By: @republicworld Russia Beyond, (Alexander Bulla)
@dindakarrtika shows us how three countries can unite for one celebration. The Fulan Fehan Festival is at Fulan Fehan, East Nusa Tenggara (NTT), Indonesia.

This Shark-Free Lagoon looks like a perfect tropical escape,  but what makes it even more fascinating is ‘what you don’t find there’.⁠
⁠
Despite being part of the Pacific Island region, this lagoon is completely safe from sharks due to its extreme salinity and shallow depth. The water is also unusually warm, creating a calm, almost surreal swimming environment.⁠
⁠
It’s one of those rare places where nature quietly creates a space that feels both beautiful and unusual at the same time.⁠
⁠
By: Mat

competitors.bio: Step into wonder and find your inner explorer with National Geographic 🌎

recentPosts.caption:
There's more to the story of Pompeii than just destruction. Join Tom Hiddleston as he traces the stories of those who experienced the eruption and those who survived.

#PompeiiOutOfTime with @twhiddleston premieres July 22 on @NatGeoTV. Streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu.

@antoni is on a quest to find the best sights, tastes, and experiences the world has to offer, and you're invited. 

#BestOfTheWorld with Antoni Porowski premieres Sunday, June 7 at 9/8c on National Geographic. Stream on @DisneyPlus and @hulu

Is this the cutest mealtime you’ve ever seen? These meerkat pups are fueling up with mom. As they grow, the clan will help the matriarch raise the next generation—a cooperative way of life that’s becoming more important as the Kalahari grows hotter and drier. 

Learn why powerful matriarchs are vital to meerkat survival at the link in bio. 

Video by @ottowhitehead

From protecting wildlife to hosting on the dance floor, @robertirwinphotography seizes every opportunity that comes his way. 💛  Dancing with the Stars: The Next Pro premieres tonight at 8/7c on @abc. Stream next day on @DisneyPlus and @hulu. 

Watch Robert’s full Nat Geo 9 interview on @natgeo's YouTube at the link in bio.

Rank America's 63 national parks by size, and Great Smoky Mountains comes in middling. But scientists have identified a stunning range of some 23,000 species there, famously including more salamander varieties than anyplace else on Earth. 

See more Wonders of America at the link in bio.

Video by @bystephanievermillion

The remains of a 55,000-year-old Neanderthal fetus offer new insight into their decline. While they were discovered in southern Germany between 1968 and 1970, the bones were not formally identified until 20 years ago. Now, National Geographic Explorer Alvise Barbieri has used this rare finding to extract and analyze ancient DNA, allowing scientists to pinpoint a population crash that shrank Neanderthal genetic diversity.

Learn more at the link in bio. 

Illustration by Alice Walczer Baldinazzo

Humans kill millions of sharks each year, and overfishing poses a major threat to their populations—especially hammerheads, since they grow and reproduce slowly. These iconic creatures are already at the brink of extinction, but successful marine protection initiatives offer a glimpse of hope for the future. 

#HammerheadSharksUpClose with @bertiegregory is now streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu.

Photographed in Egypt in 2021 by @paoloverzone, this quartzite stone sarcophagus was found in the tomb of the Egyptian pharaoh Tutankhamun. Carved into the sarcophagus are four deities who were intended to protect the young pharaoh’s body.

See more iconic images from the National Geographic archive at the link in bio.

The electric green scales of the jade palm pit viper help it blend into the damp foliage of the cloud forests of western Honduras, and have also made it into a striking symbol of conservation. These snakes are threatened by habitat loss caused by the coffee industry, but local herpers—the reptile-and-amphibian equivalent of bird-watchers—are working to save them and the cloud forests they call home.

Learn more about the jade palm pit viper at the link in bio.

Photographs and video by @jjackdev

The true story of four children who survived 40 days and 40 nights stranded in the Colombian rainforest. The siblings recount what happened in #LostInTheJungle, now streaming on @disneyplus and @hulu.

For the first time in more than 900 years, the Bayeux Tapestry—a 225-foot-long work of medieval art depicting the Norman Conquest of England—has arrived in the U.K. Transporting one of the world's most famous and fragile antiquities meant navigating numerous practical and political hurdles and required years of planning, with conservators and engineers developing a highly specialized process to protect the tapestry. 
 
Nat Geo writer Chris Heath and photographer @zed_nelson witnessed its arrival

The migratory patterns of hammerheads, who can travel thousands of miles, make it complicated to protect them.

#HammerheadSharksUpClose with @bertiegregory is now streaming on @DisneyPlus and @hulu, part of #Sharkfest on National Geographic.

competitors.bio:
A window to our world 🌎
This is a commercial channel from BBC Studios.

recentPosts.caption:
Retiring at 100 years old? Not a chance! Here’s a sneak peek of Sir David Attenborough working on the narration for the upcoming BLUE PLANET III.

#DavidAttenborough100 #DavidAttenborough #BluePlanetIII

It’s World Chimpanzee Day 🐵

Chimps share DNA with humans, but Dr Jane Goodall discovered other similarities making them our closest living relatives. They use tools, hold hands, embrace one another and use non-verbal communication like humans too. 

#EarthCapture via Shutterstock 📸

1. Patrick Rolands
2. Cheryl Ramalho
3. Nadine Wagner
4. Andrew Lilley
5. Gudkov Andrey
.
.
.
.
#WorldChimpanzeeDay #JaneGoodall #Nature

Warthog snouts are survival tools 🐗

It’s used for sniffing, digging and foraging, but they must bend their legs to reach the ground. Another creature evolved a longer snout for the same purpose, and it developed a body shape we recognise today.

Find out where to watch Evolution via the link in our bio 🔗💚

#Evolution #ChrisPackham #Nature

Every lesson comes with a risk 🐻 🎣
 
With the prime fishing grounds already claimed, a young mother and her cub head upstream into fast flowing water, where powerful rapids threaten to sweep the cub away. Will the gamble pay off, or has she pushed her luck too far? 
 
Explore the world’s few remaining untouched landscapes with Eden: Untamed Planet. Find the full series via the link in our bio 🔗

#EdenUntamedPlanet #Alaska #BearCub #Salmon

Looks can be deceptive 🌈
 
The rainbow sea slug is a marine mollusk known for a striking neon appearance. Its glowing colours are for protection warning predators they are toxic to eat.
 
#EarthCapture by @jjrheams.photography
.
.
.
.
#Seaslug #Wildlife #Nature

One of nature’s greatest acts of partnership 🪺
 
To give their chicks the best possible start, a female hornbill walls herself inside a tree hollow for months. With only a tiny opening between the, her survival and that of her family rests entirely in her partner’s care. 
 
Celebrate 20 years of Planet Earth with us. Find out where to watch the full episode via our link in bio 💚

#PlanetEarth #DavidAttenborough #PE20 #PEIII

Beware the ant army 🐜 

To consume prey, ants will swarm insects like crickets and caterpillars using sheer force of numbers. They can even overwhelm wounded chickens, goats and pigs. Nothing is off the menu… including @chrisgpackham2!

Find out where to watch Evolution via the link in our bio 🔗💚

#Evolution #ChrisPackham #Nature

You’ll never look at a tennis player’s racket arm the same way again 🎾
 
As a professional tennis player trains year after year, their skeleton adapts to the repeated impact of every shot. This causes the bones in their racket arm to become up to 20% thicker and stronger than the other and reduces the risk of breaking.
 
Watch the full clip and other videos from Secrets of the Human Body via the link in bio 🔗
 
#Tennis #Wimbeldon #RacketSport

Evolution: Official Trailer 
 
Uncover the 4-billion-year story behind five iconic animals, from their simple origins to the complex creatures we see today. Chris Packham embarks on a series of adventures through deep time, revealing how evolution shaped them in the most surprising ways.
 
Find out where to watch via the link in our bio 🔗💚

#Evolution #ChrisPackham #Nature

How did life on Earth become what it is today? 
 
Join Chris Packham on a spectacular journey through four billion years of evolution. From familiar creatures to unimaginable ancestors, this series explores simple yet powerful questions, revealing the forces that have shaped life on our planet as we know it today. 
 
Trailer drops tomorrow! 
 
#Evolution #ChrisPackham

That was a close call 🦩
 
Flamingo chicks face one of their first survival tests when the lake freezes overnight. While the adults have the strength to break free, many of the young birds are left stranded. Watch how an unexpected shift in the wind offers the remaining chicks a chance to break free and continue their journey.
 
Explore the world’s few remaining untouched landscapes with Eden: Untamed Planet. Find the full series via the link in our bio 🔗
 
#EdenUntamedPlanet #Flamingo #Extreme

🚨 Caution trip hazard 🚨

These mussel-feeding oystercatcher chicks will learn vital feeding techniques from their parents for up to 26 weeks, one of the longest feeding apprenticeships of any wading bird.

#Earthcapture by @peanut.chu.adventures
.
.
.
.
#Oystercatcher #Birds #Wadingbirds
